description: Fix 5 failing stress tests in stress-mcp-dispatch.e2e.ts. Root cause: tests fire 10-20 concurrent calls expecting all to succeed, but MAX_CONCURRENT_DISPATCHES_PER_PLUGIN=5 rejects the 6th+. Also fix hot-reload, tab-close, and permission-change tests that fail due to test setup issues (calling tools before extension is ready).

userStories.title: Fix concurrent dispatch tests to respect MAX_CONCURRENT_DISPATCHES_PER_PLUGIN=5
userStories.description: The '10 concurrent echo calls' and '20 concurrent echo calls' tests fire more calls than the per-plugin limit of 5, causing calls 6+ to be rejected with 'Too many concurrent dispatches'. Fix by either: (a) sending calls in batches of 5, or (b) testing the limit behavior explicitly (5 succeed, 6th rejected, then second batch of 5 succeeds after first batch completes).
acceptanceCriteria: The '10 concurrent echo calls' test passes — either by batching into 2 sequential batches of 5, or by asserting exactly 5 succeed and 5 get concurrency errors
The '20 concurrent echo calls' test passes — same approach
Response routing is still verified: each successful result's message matches its input (no cross-contamination)
npx playwright test e2e/stress-mcp-dispatch.e2e.ts --grep 'concurrent echo' passes on all retries
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/stress-mcp-dispatch.e2e.ts (fix the two concurrent dispatch tests)

Context:
The server has `MAX_CONCURRENT_DISPATCHES_PER_PLUGIN = 5` in `platform/mcp-server/src/mcp-tool-dispatch.ts`. When 10 calls fire concurrently, calls 6-10 are immediately rejected with 'Too many concurrent dispatches for plugin "e2e-test" (limit: 5)'.

The error at line 42/79:
```
Error: result 5 should not be an error: Too many concurrent dispatches for plugin "e2e-test" (limit: 5).
```

Fix approach — option A (recommended): Change the tests to fire calls in sequential batches of 5. First batch of 5 via Promise.all, verify all succeed with correct messages. Then second batch of 5, verify. This still tests concurrent dispatch (5 at a time) and response routing correctness.

Fix approach — option B: Test the limit explicitly. Fire 6 calls. Assert exactly 5 succeed and 1 fails with the concurrency error. Then after the 5 complete, fire 5 more — all must succeed (proves slot cleanup).

Either approach is valid. The key is: the test must reflect the ACTUAL system design (5 concurrent max), not assume unlimited concurrency.

Constraints:
- Do NOT change MAX_CONCURRENT_DISPATCHES_PER_PLUGIN — the limit is correct by design
- Response routing verification (positional message matching) must still work
- Keep the test meaningful — it should still verify concurrent dispatch correctness, just within the limit

userStories.title: Fix hot-reload during dispatch test — ensure extension is connected before tool calls
userStories.description: The hot-reload test fails because tool calls are made before the extension has fully connected and synced. Fix by adding proper waitForExtensionConnected + waitForLog('tab.syncAll received') + waitForToolResult warmup before starting the slow calls.
acceptanceCriteria: Test waits for extension connection and tab.syncAll before starting slow tool calls
A warmup echo call succeeds before the hot-reload chaos begins
The hot-reload test passes on all retries
npx playwright test e2e/stress-mcp-dispatch.e2e.ts --grep 'hot reload' passes
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/stress-mcp-dispatch.e2e.ts (fix hot-reload test)

Context:
The test uses Playwright fixtures which auto-setup the server and extension, but the tool calls may fire before the extension WebSocket is connected and tab.syncAll has been processed. The existing passing tests in dispatch-resilience.e2e.ts use `waitForExtensionConnected(server)` and `waitForLog(server, 'tab.syncAll received')` before making calls.

The test currently starts slow_with_progress calls right after opening the side panel or setting up, without confirming the dispatch pipeline is ready. Fix by adding the standard warmup sequence:
```typescript
await waitForExtensionConnected(server);
await waitForLog(server, 'tab.syncAll received', 15_000);
// Warmup: verify dispatch works before starting chaos
await waitForToolResult(mcpClient, 'e2e-test_echo', { message: 'warmup' }, { isError: false }, 15_000);
```

Constraints:
- Check whether the test uses fixtures or manual setup — add the waitFor calls accordingly
- The warmup call proves the pipeline is ready before starting the real test
- Do NOT change the hot-reload logic or the in-flight assertion logic — only fix the setup

userStories.title: Fix tab-close and permission-change dispatch tests — same warmup issue
userStories.description: The 'tab close during dispatch' and 'permission change mid-dispatch' tests fail for the same reason: tool calls fire before the dispatch pipeline is ready. Add proper extension connection + warmup before chaos.
acceptanceCriteria: Both tests have proper waitForExtensionConnected + tab.syncAll + warmup echo before chaos
The tab-close test passes: slow call interrupted by tab close returns error within 10s
The permission-change test passes: in-flight call completes, subsequent call rejected
npx playwright test e2e/stress-mcp-dispatch.e2e.ts passes on all tests
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/stress-mcp-dispatch.e2e.ts (fix tab-close and permission-change tests)

Context:
Same root cause as US-002. These tests call tools before the extension WebSocket pipeline is established. The existing fixture-based setup starts the server and extension but doesn't wait for them to fully sync.

For the permission-change test, also check that the test correctly uses `OPENTABS_DANGEROUSLY_SKIP_PERMISSIONS: ''` (empty string, not '1') to ensure permission selects are interactive and the permission system is enabled.

Add the standard warmup:
```typescript
await waitForExtensionConnected(mcpServer);
await waitForLog(mcpServer, 'tab.syncAll received', 15_000);
const warmup = await waitForToolResult(mcpClient, 'e2e-test_echo', { message: 'warmup' }, { isError: false }, 15_000);
```

Constraints:
- Both tests need warmup BEFORE the chaos starts
- The permission test may need `writeTestConfig` with explicit OPENTABS_DANGEROUSLY_SKIP_PERMISSIONS override
- Check if the fixture-based test provides access to mcpServer.logs for waitForLog
